item.title: Trump hopes for Iran deal in 2 or 3 days, but war in Lebanon continues
item.description: As Trump voices optimism for a deal with Iran within a few days, a tense truce between Israel and Iran is tested by ongoing violence in Lebanon.

item.title: Watch Live: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

item.title: Jodie Turner-Smith talks "The Agency" and being in Taylor Swift's music video
item.description: Actor Jodie Turner-Smith talks about starring in "The Agency" with Michael Fassbender and explains how she relates to her character, who is a political prisoner. She also discusses what it was like being in Taylor Swift's music video for "Opalite."

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: History and inspiration behind Uncle Sam
item.description: In the series "USA to Z," "CBS Mornings" breaks down the history of Uncle Sam and who inspired the national icon.

item.title: Young voters say both Democrats and Republicans "get it wrong"
item.description: Emma Goldberg, a reporter for The New York Times, joins CBS News 24/7 Mornings to discuss why young voters feel disillusioned by both the Republican and Democratic parties ahead of the 2026 midterm elections.

item.title: Meta launches program to train workers for data center jobs
item.description: Meta pledged to invest $115 million to train electricians, plumbers and other workers needed to operate data centers.

item.title: Alligator attacks DWI suspect who was fleeing police
item.description: A man suspected of driving while impaired was attacked by an alligator after attempting to flee police in Louisiana, authorities said.

item.title: Fans swarm MSG for Knicks-Spurs Game 3 of NBA finals
item.description: Tens of thousands of fans descended on Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of the NBA finals between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs. Security was heightened at the arena amid President Trump's attendance. CBS News' Jared Ochacher has more details.

item.title: OpenAI says it filed confidential IPO as it positions itself for AI arms race
item.description: Going public will allow OpenAI, valued at $852 billion, to inject more cash into its business as the AI race quickens.

item.title: Rob Reiner's son Nick seeks $1.5M from trust fund for defense in parents' killings
item.description: Nick Reiner is accused of stabbing his parents Rob and Michele Singer Reiner to death at their home in December.

item.title: Russia war rages on as Ukraine generals claim advances against Putin's troops
item.description: Ukrainian generals say troops are advancing in the battlefield against Russia. This comes as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he had "positive" conversations with U.S. envoys Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff. CBS News' Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: House to vote on ICE funding, ending months-long impasse
item.description: The House will be voting on Republicans' $70 billion bill to fund immigration enforcement agencies for the rest of the Trump administration.

item.title: What we know about Xi Jinping's summit with North Korea's Kim Jong Un
item.description: China's Xi Jinping concluded his summit with North Korea's Kim Jong Un where they discussed anti-American sentiments and economic opportunities. CBS News' Anna Coren reports.

item.title: Can an AI agent cover for you while you're at the beach?
item.description: "AI can make everything that was on my plate visible to colleagues while I'm gone," one expert said.

item.title: Trump's relationship with Israel's Netanyahu tested over Iran war
item.description: President Trump's relationship with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appears tense as both approach the war with Iran and the ongoing fighting with the Hezbollah militant group in Lebanon. Former Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Michael Oren joins CBS News with more insight.

item.title: Israel bombs Lebanon again despite Trump's demands to stop
item.description: Israel continues to attack parts of Lebanon as tensions with the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group remain volatile. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Platner seeks Democratic nomination to take on Sen. Collins in closely watched race
item.description: Maine Democrat Graham Platner is seeking to make the nomination official to take on Republican Sen. Susan Collins in November.

item.title: Severe storms could impact more than 90 million across multiple states
item.description: States across the Midwest and northern Plains could see ongoing flooding as severe storms hit, forecasters say.

item.title: Oprah Daily executive editor opens up about infertility, journey to motherhood and healing
item.description: Paulie Dibner, the executive editor of Oprah Daily, speaks with "CBS Mornings" about her transformative journey to motherhood after years of fertility complications and details where she found an unexpected source of healing.

item.title: Maine's Graham Platner remains defiant for Democratic primary despite Nazi tattoo, other scandals
item.description: Graham Platner remains defiant as he heads into the Maine Democratic Senate primary despite multiple controversies emerging linked to his past. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Man sustains injuries to both arms after shark attack at Navy Base marina in Florida
item.description: Officials say an employee at a Navy Base marina in Panama City, Florida, was taking a swim during his lunch break when he was attacked by a shark on Monday. The man sustained injuries to both arms and was taken to a nearby hospital.

item.title: Dogs help uncover record haul of drugs hidden in lumber
item.description: The drugs found in the lumber were mostly cocaine and ketamine, with an estimated retail value of $8.3 billion, officials said.

item.title: Latest news on Graham Platner's Maine primary and other races to watch today
item.description: Graham Platner's Maine primary to win the Democratic Senate nomination is occurring as more details about his past continue to emerge. Key races are also occurring in South Carolina, Nevada and North Dakota. CBS News' Fin Gómez, Skyler Henry and Anthony Salvanto have the latest.

item.title: Guide to mass transit at World Cup host cities across the U.S.
item.description: Eleven U.S. cities over the next few weeks will host thousands of World Cup fans. But getting to the games won't be cheap in some cities. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: First-ever U.S. sea drone rescue for Apache crew near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The cause of an Apache helicopter crash near the Strait of Hormuz that led to the U.S. military's first-ever sea drone rescue is under investigation.

item.title: How to buy SpaceX shares as its blockbuster IPO readies for liftoff
item.description: SpaceX is setting aside a large chunk of shares for ordinary investors as it seeks to raise a record $75 billion. Here's what to know.

item.title: Stellantis recalls more than 1 million Jeeps in U.S. it says could catch fire
item.description: Stellantis is recalling almost 1.08 million Jeep Wranglers and Gladiators in the U.S. that could catch fire even when they're parked and turned off, federal regulators say.

item.title: President Trump booed by MSG crowd prior to Game 3 of NBA Finals
item.description: Donald Trump was booed loudly by fans inside Madison Square Garden when he was shown on video screens during the national anthem prior to Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday.

item.title: U.S. bars entry of FIFA World Cup referee from Somalia
item.description: Federal immigration authorities barred a Somali soccer referee who was slated to officiate the FIFA World Cup from entering the U.S. over the weekend, citing "vetting concerns."
